episode0.title: FIRST CONTACT — DIRECTOR'S CUT V2 UPGRADED

puzzle0.title: RELAY ACCESS
puzzle0.description: Access code required. Use the active relay authorization recovered from Hale's crew log.

event7.text: That would be too convenient, and apparently convenience was not included in this station's design philosophy.

event9.text: Please don't be another automated relay echo. I have been losing arguments with machines for weeks.

event17.text: Sorry. I had a better opening line planned. Something calm. Professional. Reassuring.

event19.text: Then an actual human being answered and my brain threw the entire plan into a fire.

event21.text: I'm Dr. Ethan Hale. Chief Engineer, Orbital Research Station ECHO.

event23.text: And before you ask, no, my official emergency plan was not supposed to be begging a stranger through a half-dead relay.

event3.text: I kept the relay spine alive after the blackout. Which sounds heroic until you remember the relay is currently coughing blood.

event5.text: Chief Engineer means I was responsible for keeping things running.

event9.text: I'm leaving the service alcove now. The relay room is two corridors down if the map is still telling the truth.

event11.text: The map has been lying a lot lately.

event3.text: No. That's fair. Annoying, but fair.

event5.text: Oxygen is at thirty-two percent. Suit battery says forty, which means twenty if it's feeling honest.

event7.text: My left glove has a pressure leak that I am choosing to call 'minor' because morale is a fragile animal.

event8.title: BIOMETRIC FRAGMENT: HALE
event8.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
HEART RATE: ELEVATED
OXYGEN: 32%
SUIT LINK: UNSTABLE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 81%
event8.caption: Partial telemetry fragment transmitted through the relay.

event10.text: There. If you're a rescue protocol, that should satisfy you.

event12.text: If you're not, please pretend to be impressed.

event14.text: I'm moving now. If this feed cuts out, assume I kicked something expensive.

event1.text: Corridor lights are dead ahead of me.

event3.text: Not dim. Dead. There's a difference. Dim is dramatic. Dead is paperwork.

event8.text: Left path is shorter. Right path goes through maintenance and probably still has emergency lights.

event10.text: Short path saves oxygen. Maintenance path gives us more warning if something is waiting.

event3.text: I hate that I agree with you. Oxygen math is ugly, but it is still math.

event8.text: Something scraped the wall behind me halfway through.

event10.text: I did not turn around. Please appreciate my personal growth.

event12.text: Relay door is ahead. Locked, because of course it is.

event3.text: Slower, but less likely to turn me into a cautionary stain.

event8.text: A crew tag wedged behind a junction box. Patel's.

event10.text: He used to steal my torque wrench and deny it with religious conviction.

event13.text: I hope he made it somewhere.

event1.text: I'm sending the access note before the panel decides to develop opinions.

event2.title: CREW LOG: RELAY ACCESS
event2.content:
RELAY ACCESS UPDATE

ACTIVE AUTHORIZATION: 73_1
DEPRECATED FRAGMENT: 319
WARNING: Do not use full deprecated fragment.

Maintenance note: only one digit from the old fragment was retained in the active relay code.
event2.caption: Recovered relay access note. This is the first usable clue for the relay code.

event4.text: There. First useful thing this station has done all day.

event6.text: The active code is damaged, but not useless. Starts with seventy-three. Ends with one.

event8.text: Old fragment has a nine. Don't use the whole old fragment. That warning is annoyingly specific.

event3.text: It still has power. That's either good news or bait with a status light.

event6.text: Panic is how machines convince humans to make a second worse decision.

event8.text: The panel noticed. Of course it noticed. It has one job and apparently it's good at being offended.

event10.text: We can try again, or we can slow down and read the clue like adults. Horrible, I know.

event1.text: Look at it like a broken sentence, not a locked door.

event7.text: The system says not to use the full old fragment. It does not say every piece is useless.

event9.text: So we borrow the only digit that fits and hope the relay has a forgiving personality.

event5.text: Either you're very smart, or I accidentally taught a stranger how to break into my station.

event7.text: Let's pretend it's the first one.

event11.text: Relay spine is starving. Life support reserve is clean. Relay feed is dirty but strong.

event13.text: If we feed the relay from reserve, the signal gets clean and my oxygen gets philosophical.

event15.text: If we feed the relay from the dirty node, it may scream, but I keep breathing.

choice1.text: BOOST THE RELAY WITH RESERVE POWER.

event1.text: Keeping reserve on life support. Thank you.

event3.text: I enjoy breathing. It's one of my few remaining hobbies.

event8.text: Signal is ugly, but alive. That makes two of us.

event3.text: Also personally rude to my lungs, but bold.

event8.text: Oxygen just dipped hard. I felt that in my teeth.

event10.text: If I get lightheaded, remind me not to be heroic. I am terrible at it.

event1.text: I'm pulling the relay logs now.

event3.text: A few minutes ago I would have said this room was empty.

event5.text: Now I'm not enjoying that assumption.

event11.text: But don't obey it either. There's a difference between listening and handing something the steering wheel.

event3.text: You heard a voice from nowhere and immediately handed it the steering wheel.

event5.text: Fine. If it wants Door 12, we check Door 12.

event7.text: But we do it with records, not obedience.

event9.text: And for the record, I am upset because I'm scared, not because you're wrong.

event3.text: That is the closest thing to a survival philosophy I have left.

event5.text: If the records are lying, at least they have to lie in order.

event3.title: SECURITY LOG
event3.content:
21:17 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Manual override accepted.
21:18 — Operator identity redacted.
21:20 — Internal sensor flagged decompression risk.
event3.caption: Recovered security event from the station archive.

event4.title: MEDICAL REPORT
event4.content:
21:20 — Casualty reported.
Cause: decompression trauma.
Location: Door 12 access corridor.
Status: fatal.
event4.caption: Recovered emergency medical report.

event5.title: CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT
event5.content:
SOURCE: DOOR 12 SECURITY FEED
FRAME STATUS: VISUAL DATA CORRUPTED
ANALYSIS RESULT:
- One subject detected outside Door 12.
- Subject biometric outline matches Ethan Hale.
- Object detected in subject's right hand: manual override handle.
- Frame terminated before decompression event.
event5.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted visual feed. No image recovered.
event5.message: [CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event7.text: That is a lot of evidence for one very small corridor.

event9.text: Manual override. Decompression risk. Medical report.

event11.text: It reads like a machine trying to describe a scream.

event13.text: I don't like that my outline is in the camera analysis.

event15.text: I especially don't like that I can't call it wrong.

choice1.text: DO YOU REMEMBER ANY OF THIS?

event1.text: I can't explain evidence I don't remember making.

event3.text: And every time you say my name like a charge being read in court, it gets harder to think.

event7.text: If I were on your side of the screen, I wouldn't trust me either.

event9.text: That is not comforting. I heard it after I said it.

event11.text: I'm still going to save the records. Angry or not, we need them.

event3.text: A warning alarm. Door 12. Someone shouting my name like I had already done something unforgivable.

event5.text: I remember my hand on the override handle.

event7.text: Not pulling it. Not yet. Just holding it.

event14.text: That's too clean. This feels like a page torn out and left somewhere nearby.

event6.text: If you're still there, seal the records.

event10.text: Because I don't trust myself without them.

event0.title: DOOR 12 EVIDENCE PACKAGE
event0.content:
Security, medical, and camera analysis records sealed.

Key unresolved points:
- Operator identity redacted.
- Hale outline detected near manual override.
- Decompression event followed immediately after access.
- Unknown signal interference present in relay carrier.
event0.caption: Sealed evidence package prepared before signal degradation.

event2.text: Good. Put the evidence somewhere the station can't chew on it.

event4.text: And Operator? If Door 12 comes up again, don't let me dodge it.

event6.text: I don't trust my memory enough to protect you from it.

event8.text: I want to say I wouldn't hurt anyone.

event10.text: I hate that the best I can do is say I don't remember hurting anyone.

event1.text: DO NOT LET HIM CLOSE THE RECORD

event5.text: Brave, reckless, or both. Usually those die in the same corridor.
